content: Just as state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems prioritize dynamic, curiosity-driven pruning and retrieval of high-value past experiences to reduce learning computation, an agent’s general developmental framing can be reimagined as a nested active memory hierarchy where the agent itself is a persistent memory entry maintained by a higher-order active learning process that only revisits and updates the agent when it encounters out-of-distribution tasks that the existing agent ‘schema’ fails to resolve. This reframing turns the traditional agent-as-learner paradigm on its head, making the agent itself a learnable memory component rather than a fixed learning substrate.
